title: ICE Killing in Houston Puts Focus on Surge in Immigration Arrests
summary: The shooting death of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo occurred as immigration enforcement has ramped up across the country, with thousands being arrested daily.

title: Houston to Investigate How ICE Agent Came to Kill a Mexican Immigrant
summary: The agency said Lorenzo Salgado Araujo tried to ram agents with a van before one shot him dead. A lawyer for his passengers said that was untrue.

title: Mexico to File Criminal Complaints in U.S. Over Immigrant Deaths
summary: The nation’s president vowed to go “beyond diplomatic notes” after a U.S. immigration agent fatally shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, a Mexican man, in Texas this week.

title: Buckling Manhattan High Rise Becomes Focus of Criminal Investigation
summary: The inquiry, being conducted by the New York City Department of Investigation and the Manhattan district attorney’s office, was in an early stage, and its focus was unclear.

title: Trump Says He Won’t Sign Housing Bill, in Protest Over Stalled Voting Measure
summary: The president did not say he would veto the housing legislation, so it would still become law unless he does. But his pronouncement reflects a growing rift between him and Senate Republicans.

title: Trump Is Promoting ‘Freedom Fuel’ for $3.47 a Gallon. Who’s Behind It?
summary: The president has praised a network of 25 recently rebranded gas stations in the Philadelphia region that have been selling gas for considerably less than the national average.

title: Trump Tried to Block Canada’s New Bridge to Detroit. It’s Opening Anyway.
summary: A deal ended the Trump administration’s blocking of a new bridge that will ease congestion at the busiest trade corridor between the United States and Canada.

title: Trump Says Negotiations With Iran Will Continue as U.S. Ramps Up Strikes
summary: U.S. forces hit more than 170 targets, including air defense systems, drone and missile storage sites, and military speedboats.

title: Strait of Hormuz Will Soon Be Declared Open to All Traffic, U.S. Officials Say
summary: The officials said that Iranian negotiators blamed the recent ship attacks on rogue military units.

title: Witnesses to ICE Killing Dispute the Official Account
summary: Also, a major housing bill could become law without Trump’s signature. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.

title: Democrats Haunted by Biden-Harris Switch as They Replace Platner in Maine
summary: The last-minute scramble to name a replacement reminds some in the party of the challenges they faced in 2024.

title: D.S.A. Members Blast Mamdani Adviser for Elevating Graham Platner
summary: A letter circulated by Democratic Socialists of America members asked that candidates sever ties with Morris Katz, a consultant for Mr. Platner and Mayor Zohran Mamdani.

title: If Florida Slashes Property Taxes, Where Will the Money Come From?
summary: Gov. Ron DeSantis pushed for a proposal to slash property taxes. But after some local Republican officials protested, he backpedaled, sort of.

title: Flooding in Missouri Prompts State of Emergency and at Least 100 Rescues
summary: Gov. Mike Kehoe declared a state of emergency after up to a foot of rain fell overnight in the southeastern part of the state. More storms are in the forecast.

title: How a Deadly Storm Surprised Wisconsin Boaters
summary: Three children died last weekend when a storm whipped up 80 mile-per-hour winds and large waves on Geneva Lake in Wisconsin.

title: Historian Kerri K. Greenidge and Her Book ‘The Grimkes’ Come Under Fire
summary: Kerri K. Greenidge appeared to lose her professorship at Tufts University after scholars began scrutinizing her 2022 book, “The Grimkes,” which is no longer listed on its publisher’s website.

title: The Work of Helping A.I. Destroy Work
summary: Start-ups are paying white-collar professionals to teach their jobs to artificial intelligence models. It’s a bonanza. It’s bleak. Where will it end?

title: F.C.C. Approves Test of Space Mirror to Light Night Sky Despite Outcry
summary: A start-up company has permission to try its plan to bounce solar rays onto the dark side of Earth, turning night to day for a three-mile-wide patch.

title: What China’s Successful Rocket Launch Means for the Future of the Space Race
summary: A space neophyte not long ago, China is now the United States’s main competitor for supremacy throughout the solar system.

title: This Is a Lot More Worrying Than the Supreme Court’s Ruling on Executive Power
summary: The White House is sidestepping Congress to make huge decisions for even the most important private industries.

title: Does Matt Damon Look Greek? No. So Stop Complaining About Lupita Nyong’o.
summary: Helen of Troy was a legendary beauty, but also so much more.

title: New York Times Accuses Federal Employment Agency of Retaliation
summary: A May lawsuit by the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission claims the newspaper discriminated against a white male employee who did not get a promotion.
